description: signWebhook prepends a secret to the payload before hashing (MAC); hashAsset and hashIdempotency hash the payload alone.

findings.rationale: signWebhook is structurally near-identical to hashAsset and hashIdempotency (single sha256hex call, string return) but prefixes the secret before hashing. The prefix is load-bearing: without it the output is an unauthenticated content hash, not a MAC, and any party with the payload can forge a matching signature. An agent that normalises the three onto a shared hash() will drop the secret prefix unless warned.

tier_note: Requires structural+data-flow detector; beyond regex detector scope.
